🌆 Market News Digest
[Jul 21, 2026 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• Oil spikes on Middle East escalation — Brent topped $91 as U.S.-Iran tensions, Houthi threats and tanker disruptions lifted energy prices and inflation fears
• Trump ups pressure on Iran — said strikes/blockade actions may continue, while U.K. bases reportedly approved for some U.S. strikes
• U.S. stocks rally on AI/chip rebound — Nasdaq led gains as NVDA, MU, TSM and semis surged on renewed AI optimism
• Trade war risk rises — Trump said new tariffs on dozens of countries could land this week, with Canada talks still tense
• Hormuz/shipping disruptions deepen — tanker U-turns, drone attacks and airport closures signaled wider regional spillover

⛽ Oil & Energy
• Brent $91, WTI $84.91 — crude settled up ~2% on supply-risk headlines; Red Sea/Hormuz traffic hit multi-week lows
• Saudi crude tankers reverse course — Houthi naval blockade threats and Iran-linked attacks rattled shipping routes
• Iraq-U.S. oil deal chatter — Iraq said U.S. company agreements could total $200B and boost gas/oil output

📊 Markets & Macro
• Equities finish higher — Dow +0.72%, S&P 500 +0.88%, Nasdaq +1.33% as AI breadth returned
• Yen at 163 vs $ — weakest since 1986, raising intervention risk
• Rates/liq. watch — Fed seen holding steady Jul 29; reverse repo use plunged, implying ample liquidity
• Macro prints mixed — Philly Fed non-mfg rebounded sharply; ADP weekly jobs slowed to 16.5K

🌍 Geopolitical
• Mideast conflict widens — Kuwait/Bahrain reported air raid sirens and drone activity; Erbil airport briefly shut after attacks
• Lebanon diplomacy — Trump/Aoun meeting ended with promises of U.S. support and direct U.S.-Lebanon flight access
• Ukraine reshuffle — Zelensky dismissed army chief Syrskyi and named Drapatyi, signaling a military reset
• Russia crypto law — parliament passed a framework with a $3,800 annual cap for retail investors

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• Crypto bill advances — Bessent said the U.S. Crypto Clarity Act is at the “1-yard line,” with White House pressure on Senate Democrats
• Tariff/forced-labor actions — U.S. to impose new tariffs on trading partners by Friday, citing forced labor
• France bans social media for under-15s — first in Europe to do so, a major online policy shift
• Hungary cuts rates — 25 bps to 5.75%, in line with expectations

🏢 Corporate
• Strong bank/insurance earnings — Schwab, Capital One and Marsh McLennan topped estimates, pointing to solid consumer/asset flows
• Big industrial beats — 3M, GM and Genuine Parts beat on EPS/sales and mostly raised or reaffirmed guidance
• AI capex stays hot — Google launched new Gemini models; OpenAI said agents hit 10M users; MS/Mistral expanded Europe infra
• Semis keep running — Micron +10%+ on BofA boost; TSMC may raise prices up to 10% in 2027; Nvidia says Vera Rubin is on track
• Apple pushes financing — preparing “Apple Upgrade” leasing program with Klarna to support device sales
